items.title: Iran war day 114: US, Iranian delegations in Switzerland for key talks
items.standfirst: Lebanon to top the agenda as US and Iran to hold talks in Switzerland’s Burgenstock mediated by Qatar and Pakistan.
items.content: Lebanon to top the agenda as US and Iran to hold talks in Switzerland’s Burgenstock mediated by Qatar and Pakistan.

items.title: Middle East live: US Vice President JD Vance arrives in Switzerland for Iran peace talks
items.standfirst: US Vice President JD Vance landed Sunday in Switzerland for a new round of negotiations with Iranian leadership over the Middle East war, even as Tehran said it was closing the Strait of Hormuz again after Israeli attacks in Lebanon. The framework was signed last week, and negotiators are now in a 60-day sprint to reach an agreement on the technical details. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: US Vice President JD Vance landed Sunday in Switzerland for a new round of negotiations with Iranian leadership over the Middle East war, even as Tehran said it was closing the Strait of Hormuz again after Israeli attacks in Lebanon. The framework was signed last week, and negotiators are now in a 60-day sprint to reach an agreement on the technical details. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iran says it is closing strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Unclear if threat has been carried out or if move will jeopardise talks with US scheduled for SundayIran has said it is closing the strait of Hormuz after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon in a move that threatens to derail the fragile interim peace deal with the US, signed just days ago.The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned ships not to approach the strategic waterway, which before the war carried a fifth of global oil and liquid gas supplies, citing what it called Israeli crimes in Lebanon and a US violation of commitments to establish a ceasefire there. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran says closed Strait of Hormuz as US deal hits obstacle ahead of Swiss talks
items.standfirst: Iran said Saturday it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israel’s military campaign in Lebanon, while warning that upcoming talks with the United States in Switzerland are unlikely to advance unless the fighting stops. President Donald Trump countered by threatening U.S.-imposed tolls on shipping through the strategic waterway if a final agreement is not reached within 60 days.
items.content: Iran said Saturday it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israel’s military campaign in Lebanon, while warning that upcoming talks with the United States in Switzerland are unlikely to advance unless the fighting stops. President Donald Trump countered by threatening U.S.-imposed tolls on shipping through the strategic waterway if a final agreement is not reached within 60 days.

items.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire after continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Iran has closed the strait of Hormuz – citing alleged ⁠violations of a ceasefire agreement ​by the US ‌and Israel. Tehran said the closure was the "first step" in response ​to what it described ​as breaches ​of commitments regarding the continuing clashes in Lebanon - and warned ​that further measures would be taken if "aggression" continued. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani, and FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.
items.content: Iran has closed the strait of Hormuz – citing alleged ⁠violations of a ceasefire agreement ​by the US ‌and Israel. Tehran said the closure was the "first step" in response ​to what it described ​as breaches ​of commitments regarding the continuing clashes in Lebanon - and warned ​that further measures would be taken if "aggression" continued. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani, and FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah renew ceasefire after deadly flareup disrupts opening of Iran talks
items.standfirst: At least five killed in Israeli air strike in southern Lebanon on Saturday morning, as US envoy Steve Witkoff reportedly prepares to open talks with Iranian foreign ministerWhat lessons will Iran’s new leadership draw from the 110-day war?‘It’s a big mistake’: Israelis feel betrayed and angry after Iran peace dealIsrael and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and Iran to end their conflict.A meeting that was scheduled to take place on Friday between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland to discuss implementation of the new deal was cancelled when Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 47 people. Continue reading...

items.title: Zelensky returns Polish award as WWII row strains alliance
items.standfirst: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.
items.content: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.

items.title: Poland strips Zelensky of top state honour over WWII dispute
items.standfirst: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.
items.content: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.

Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Germany book knockout spot with comeback win over Ivory Coast

Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.

clusters.title: The happiest World Cup game
bullet_summary: Reported by Politico, Al Jazeera

items.title: The happiest World Cup game
items.content: POLITICO has been crunching the numbers to see how all 48 of this year’s World Cup participants rank in several other off-field categories, which we’ll share more of over the weekend. In today’s item, we're looking at countries sorted by their FIFA rank against their citizens' assessment of how close they are to living their best possible life. Turns out, the happiest game of the World Cup will be Sweden vs. the Netherlands today (that’s also the only group-stage game taking place between two EU members) — while the upcoming game between Haiti and Morocco on June 25 may well be interrupted by floods of tears and bouts of introspection.

items.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae bloom turn water green
items.standfirst: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.
items.content: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

clusters.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
clusters.neutral_headline: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
bullet_summary: Reported by Wired, Reuters Top News

items.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
items.standfirst: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.
items.content: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.

items.title: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
items.standfirst: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]
items.content: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]

clusters.title: Trump unveils new converted Air Force One jet he accepted as gift from Qatar
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump unveils new converted Air Force One jet he accepted as gift from Qatar
bullet_summary: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet
Reported by Sky News World, CBS News

items.title: Trump unveils new converted Air Force One jet he accepted as gift from Qatar
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has unveiled the new Air Force One, an upgraded Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar that has been converted into the official US presidential aircraft.
items.content: Donald Trump has unveiled the new Air Force One, an upgraded Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar that has been converted into the official US presidential aircraft.

items.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a $400 million plane gifted by Qatar
items.standfirst: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.
items.content: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

clusters.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
clusters.neutral_headline: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.standfirst: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.
items.content: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference
items.standfirst: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
